questions.text: In communication, information is encoded and imparted by sender to receiver via a/an
questions.explanation: The channel or medium is the means through which information travels from sender to receiver.

questions.text: The researcher in a communication process after decoding the message, gives the sender a
questions.explanation: Feedback is the response given by the receiver back to the sender after decoding.

questions.text: Communication requires that all parties involved in that process have common background
questions.explanation: Effective communication requires common background/shared understanding between parties.

questions.text: The most common types of communication are:
questions.explanation: Verbal and non-verbal are the two most common broad types of communication.

questions.text: Verbal communication has to do with when communication is verbalized or spoken
questions.explanation: Verbal communication involves the use of words, whether spoken or written.

questions.text: The process of communicating through sending and receiving wordless message is referred to as
questions.explanation: Non-verbal communication involves wordless messages through body language, gestures, etc.

questions.text: Non-verbal communication plays a key role in every person's day to day life
questions.explanation: Non-verbal cues like facial expressions and body language are essential in daily interactions.

questions.text: One of the following is not a type of communication
questions.explanation: Engineering communication is not a recognized type of communication.

questions.text: The type of communication that is done through visual aid is
questions.explanation: Visual communication uses visual aids like charts, graphs, and images.

questions.text: The type of communication that relies on the organ of smell which is the nose is
questions.explanation: Chemical communication relies on the sense of smell (pheromones, etc).

questions.text: Maintaining a distance between us and our friends could communicate our feelings to them
questions.explanation: Physical distance is a form of non-verbal communication that conveys feelings.

questions.text: Stimulus in communication is seen as the trigger that triggers the communication process
questions.explanation: A stimulus is the event or trigger that initiates the communication process.

questions.text: An encoder/sender in a communication process refers to the individual who also begins the exchange
questions.explanation: The encoder (sender) initiates the communication by encoding and sending a message.

questions.text: The following are elements of communication process except
questions.explanation: Form/chemical is not a recognized element of the communication process.

questions.text: The decoder is also called the target or receiver
questions.explanation: The decoder is the person who receives and interprets the message, also called the receiver.

questions.text: The result that the encoder gets about the message that he sends across to the receiver is
questions.explanation: Feedback is the response the encoder receives about the message sent.

questions.text: Communication could be vertical or horizontal
questions.explanation: Communication flows can be vertical (up/down hierarchy) or horizontal (same level).

questions.text: The flow of information from the top to the bottom in communication is referred to as:
questions.explanation: Vertical communication flows from top to bottom (or bottom to top) in an organization.

questions.text: Horizontal communication could be referred to as the exchange of messages and ideas or feelings between two people of the same level or mates
questions.explanation: Horizontal communication occurs between people at the same organizational level.

questions.text: One of the following is not a language skill:
questions.explanation: Jumping is a physical activity, not a language skill.

questions.text: The most basic form of interpersonal communication is
questions.explanation: Dyadic communication (between two people) is the most basic form of interpersonal communication.

questions.text: Grammar is seen as the study of how words and their component parts combine to form sentences
questions.explanation: Grammar is the systematic study of how words combine to form sentences.

questions.text: The two categories of word classes in English language are:
questions.explanation: Word classes are categorized into open classes (nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs) and closed systems.

questions.text: The term parts of speech is traced to traditional grammarians
questions.explanation: The term 'parts of speech' originated from traditional grammarians.

questions.text: Nouns that designate specific people, places, states, days, festivals, rivers etc are known as:
questions.explanation: Proper nouns name specific entities and are always capitalized.

questions.text: Common nouns are divided into
questions.explanation: Common nouns are classified as countable (book, chair) or uncountable (water, air).

questions.text: Common nouns are names given to class of people, animals, place etc
questions.explanation: Common nouns refer to general categories rather than specific entities.

questions.text: The nouns that are considered as mass in nature and cannot be counted are referred to as:
questions.explanation: Uncountable nouns cannot be counted individually (e.g., water, information).

questions.text: All the following are examples of collective nouns except
questions.explanation: Boy is a common noun, not a collective noun. Collective nouns refer to groups.

questions.text: Nouns that do not exist physically and so cannot be seen or touched are:
questions.explanation: Abstract nouns refer to ideas, qualities, or conditions (love, courage, happiness).

questions.text: The head of department is a woman. The underlined expression is noun as
questions.explanation: 'The head of department' is the subject performing the action of the sentence.

questions.text: I saw him coming from school. The underlined word is noun as:
questions.explanation: 'Him' functions as a noun in a prepositional-like construction.

questions.text: The plural of the noun goose is
questions.explanation: Goose is an irregular noun; its plural is geese.

questions.text: The following as compound nouns form their plurals by adding plural markers to the first nouns except:
questions.explanation: Assistant-director forms its plural by adding -s to 'director', not 'assistant'.

questions.text: Words that can be used to replace nouns perfectly in all situations are
questions.explanation: Pronouns are words that substitute for nouns in sentences.

questions.text: There are............ persons under the personal pronoun
questions.explanation: Personal pronouns have three persons: first, second, and third person.

questions.text: The types of pronouns that denote possession are called
questions.explanation: Possessive pronouns show ownership (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs).

questions.text: Pronouns that reflect or mirror back on their subjects are
questions.explanation: Reflexive pronouns refer back to the subject (myself, yourself, himself).

questions.text: The man killed himself is an example of:
questions.explanation: 'Himself' is a reflexive pronoun reflecting back to 'the man'.

questions.text: Where is the money you promised to give me is an example of
questions.explanation: 'Where' is an interrogative pronoun used to ask questions.

questions.text: Vanessa and Benjamin like each other is an example of
questions.explanation: 'Each other' is a reciprocal pronoun showing mutual action.

questions.text: Demonstrative pronouns are
questions.explanation: Demonstrative pronouns point to specific things (this, that, these, those).

questions.text: No one is in the class is an example of
questions.explanation: 'No one' is an indefinite pronoun that does not refer to a specific person.

questions.text: A word or group of words that denotes an action or shows the state of being of the subject in a sentence is called
questions.explanation: A verb expresses action or state of being of the subject.

questions.text: The two main classes of verbs in the English language are:
questions.explanation: Verbs are classified into main (lexical) verbs and auxiliary (helping) verbs.

questions.text: Verbs whose actions as denoted move across from the subject to the object are:
questions.explanation: Transitive verbs require an object to receive the action.

questions.text: Intransitive verbs are those types of verbs which do not require objects to be fully operational
questions.explanation: Intransitive verbs do not take objects (e.g., sleep, run, arrive).

questions.text: Auxiliary verbs are also called
questions.explanation: Auxiliary verbs help the main verb (e.g., is, am, are, have, will).

questions.text: Auxiliary verbs are sub-divided into two groups
questions.explanation: Auxiliaries split into primary (be, have, do) and modal (can, will, may).

questions.text: The class of words that modify, describe or qualify verbs is called
questions.explanation: Adverbs modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs.

questions.text: Adjectives traditionally describe or modify nouns or pronouns in sentences.
questions.explanation: Adjectives modify nouns and pronouns in sentences.

questions.text: All the following are functions of adjectives except
questions.explanation: Adjectives cannot function as subjects of a sentence.

questions.text: The following are degrees of comparison of adjectives except
questions.explanation: There is no negative degree of comparison for adjectives.

questions.text: The part of speech that shows the relationship between two words in a sentence is called
questions.explanation: Prepositions show relationships between words (on, at, in, under).

questions.text: The word class that links words, groups of words, phrases, clauses and sentences is:
questions.explanation: Conjunctions link words and clauses together (and, but, or).

questions.text: The following are types of conjunctions except
questions.explanation: Superior conjunctions is not a type of conjunction.

questions.text: Either the man or his wife will attend the party is an example of
questions.explanation: 'Either...or' is a correlative conjunction pair.

questions.text: ___________ are used to express sudden outbursts of feelings or strong emotions
questions.explanation: Interjections express sudden emotions (oh!, wow!, ouch!).

questions.text: Intensive reading is usually done with a high degree of concentration
questions.explanation: Intensive reading involves careful, detailed reading with full concentration.

questions.text: The SQ3R is
questions.explanation: SQ3R is a study technique: Survey, Question, Read, Recite, Review.

questions.text: The Q in SQ3R study techniques stands for
questions.explanation: Q stands for Question in the SQ3R method.

questions.text: Cramming can lead to mental fatigue and confusion
questions.explanation: Cramming overloads the brain and leads to fatigue and poor retention.

questions.text: The following except one are factors in memory
questions.explanation: Cramming is not a positive factor in memory; it hinders retention.

questions.text: The following are genres of literature except
questions.explanation: Reading is a skill, not a genre of literature.

questions.text: Prose in literature is divided into
questions.explanation: Prose is divided into fiction (imagined) and non-fiction (factual).

questions.text: The form of literature which imitates human action is
questions.explanation: Drama imitates human action through performance.

questions.text: The term prose is derived from Latin word
questions.explanation: Prose comes from Latin 'prorsus' meaning straightforward.

questions.text: The following but one are different types of drama
questions.explanation: Musicology is the study of music, not a type of drama.

questions.text: Literature serves as a means of education
questions.explanation: Literature educates by teaching moral values and life lessons.

questions.text: Literature improves audiences use of language
questions.explanation: Reading literature enhances vocabulary and language skills.

questions.text: The following are listening defects except
questions.explanation: Sleeping factors are not classified as listening defects.

questions.text: The following are some methods to adopt while listening except:
questions.explanation: Fighting the speaker is not a proper listening method.

questions.text: __________ cannot function as main verbs
questions.explanation: Modal verbs (can, will, may) cannot function as main verbs alone.

questions.text: Tongue, Lungs, vocal cords, and lips are referred to as
questions.explanation: These are the speech organs used to produce speech sounds.

questions.text: __________ are located in the human head with the exception of the lungs
questions.explanation: Speech organs are mostly in the head; lungs are in the chest.

questions.text: The mobile organs of speech are called_______
questions.explanation: Articulators are the mobile organs of speech (tongue, lips, etc).

questions.text: Examples of the articulators are
questions.explanation: Tongue, uvula, and lips are examples of mobile articulators.

questions.text: There _________ points of articulation in speech sounds
questions.explanation: There are eight points of articulation for speech sounds.

questions.text: Sounds produced with lower lip and teeth are called__________
questions.explanation: Labiodental sounds use the lower lip and upper teeth (f, v).

questions.text: Alveolar sounds are produced with the tongue on the alveolar ridge.
questions.explanation: Alveolar sounds (t, d, n, l) use the tongue tip on the alveolar ridge.

questions.text: ________ Sounds are produced at the velum or soft palate
questions.explanation: Velar sounds (k, g, ng) are produced at the soft palate.

questions.text: Bilabial sounds are produced with both lips
questions.explanation: Bilabial sounds (p, b, m) use both lips.

questions.text: The sound produced with the tip of the tongue and both upper and lower teeth is called__________
questions.explanation: Dental sounds use the tongue tip against the teeth (th sounds).

questions.text: In the manners of articulation in speech sounds_________ forms of air blockage are identified
questions.explanation: Seven manners of articulation are identified in speech sounds.

questions.text: _________ are sounds characterized by friction of the exhaling air
questions.explanation: Fricatives produce friction as air passes through (f, v, s, z, sh).

questions.text: Sounds that are produced in the nasal cavity are
questions.explanation: Nasal sounds (m, n, ng) produce sound through the nasal cavity.

questions.text: One of these is an example of Bilabial
questions.explanation: [p] and [b] are bilabial stops produced with both lips.

questions.text: Love, anger, beauty, poverty, etc are examples of
questions.explanation: These are abstract nouns - they cannot be physically touched or seen.

questions.text: ________ constitute the sound category into which speech sounds are classified phonetically.
questions.explanation: Vowels and consonants are the two main sound categories.

questions.text: The basic types of reading disorders are:
questions.explanation: Dyslexia (difficulty reading) and alexia (loss of reading ability) are reading disorders.

questions.text: _________ refers to a difficulty with reading and writing
questions.explanation: Dyslexia is a learning disorder affecting reading ability.

questions.text: _________ and ________ are types of reading
questions.explanation: Extensive (wide, fast) and intensive (careful, detailed) are the two types.

questions.text: ___________ refers to reading widely and fast
questions.explanation: Extensive reading involves reading widely and quickly for general understanding.

questions.text: Skimming and scanning are basic skills needed for extensive reading.
questions.explanation: Skimming and scanning are essential skills for extensive reading.

questions.text: A fast skimmer may read 800 words per minute with just 5% comprehension.
questions.explanation: Skimming trades depth of comprehension for speed.

questions.text: Looking for a specific point or information while reading is called
questions.explanation: Scanning involves searching text for specific information.

questions.text: Lying on the bed is a good reading habit.
questions.explanation: Lying on the bed is not a good reading habit; it causes drowsiness and poor posture.

questions.text: The following are reading faults except
questions.explanation: Progression is normal reading movement, not a fault.

questions.text: The purpose of skimming is to ____________
questions.explanation: Skimming quickly identifies main ideas and general content.

questions.text: The environment where one reads can impede comprehension while reading
questions.explanation: A noisy or distracting environment negatively affects reading comprehension.

questions.text: Listening is_____________
questions.explanation: Listening is considered the first language skill acquired.

questions.text: Listening can be considered as a
questions.explanation: Listening is a receptive skill - you receive and process information.

questions.text: To listen well, the listener should possess the following health fitness except
questions.explanation: Intelligence is not specifically a health requirement for good listening.

questions.text: Listening impediments include the following except one:
questions.explanation: Distractions from the audience is not a standard category of listening impediment.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a type of listening
questions.explanation: Creative listening is not a recognized type of listening.

questions.text: Listening is the ability
questions.explanation: Listening encompasses receiving, interpreting, and organizing messages.
options: accurately receive and interpret message in the communication process

questions.text: Listening is the same as hearing
questions.explanation: Listening involves active processing while hearing is passive sound reception.

questions.text: Generally we have two types of listening
questions.explanation: Discriminative and comprehensive are the two main types of listening.

questions.text: The speech organs are:
questions.explanation: The nose is one of the speech organs used for nasal sounds.

questions.text: There are__________ cavities which serve as resonance chambers where sounds are resonated to take their sound value.
questions.explanation: There are 2 resonance cavities: oral and nasal.

questions.text: Sounds are either oral or nasal
questions.explanation: Speech sounds are classified as either oral (through mouth) or nasal (through nose).

questions.text: The mobile organs of speech are called____________
questions.explanation: Articulators are the mobile organs of speech.

questions.text: All these are mobile organs of speech except one:
questions.explanation: Teeth are stationary organs of speech, not mobile.

questions.text: The stationary organs of speech include the following except
questions.explanation: The uvula is a mobile organ, not a stationary one.

questions.text: ___________ numbers of speech sounds are classified according to their points of articulation
questions.explanation: 8 speech sounds are classified by their points of articulation.

questions.text: Eight positions of speech sounds identified are_________
questions.explanation: The eight include bilabial, labiodental, dental, alveolar, palatal, velar, uvular, glottal.

questions.text: All of these are forms of air blockage: I. stops II. fricatives III. affricates IV. nasal V. Lateral VI. Liquid VII. Semi-vowel
questions.explanation: All listed are recognized forms of air blockage in speech.

questions.text: The sound system of the English language involves two main features
questions.explanation: English sound system involves consonants and vowels.

questions.text: Consonant sounds are_________ in number
questions.explanation: English has 24 consonant sounds.

questions.text: We have________ vowel sounds
questions.explanation: English has 20 vowel sounds (12 pure vowels and 8 diphthongs).

questions.text: In the production of consonant sound, there is_________
questions.explanation: Consonants involve complete or partial obstruction of airflow.
options: Partial obstruction of air in the oral cavity
Complete or partial obstruction of air in the oral cavity

questions.text: Phonetically, vowels are articulated without any obstruction in the mouth
questions.explanation: Vowels are produced with free airflow and no obstruction.

questions.text: There are _______ pure vowels
questions.explanation: English has 12 pure (monophthong) vowels.

questions.text: Diphthongs are_________ in number:
questions.explanation: English has 8 diphthongs (gliding vowels).

questions.text: What is a syllable?
questions.explanation: A syllable is the minimal unit of speech, typically centered on a vowel.
options: It is a minimal unit of speech production, normally composed of vowel or vowel like consonant
It is the largest grammatical unit that contains a subject and a verb

questions.text: A syllable consists of_________ surrounded by one or more consonants usually before the vowel, but sometimes after it.
questions.explanation: The nucleus of a syllable is always a vowel or vowel-like sound.

questions.text: English has a considerable number of syllable types. They include all these except one
questions.explanation: VVV is not a standard syllable type in English.

questions.text: Apart from vowel, which other consonants can form peak or prominence?
questions.explanation: Sonorant consonants (like l, m, n) can form syllable peaks.

questions.text: A vowel can occur at the following position
questions.explanation: Vowels can appear in initial, medial, or final positions in English.
options: The initial, the medial and the final position

questions.text: The nouns that designate specific people are called:
questions.explanation: Proper nouns designate specific people; mass nouns like names can also apply.

questions.text: A common noun names
questions.explanation: Common nouns name general categories of people, animals, places, things.
options: Class of people, animals, places and so on

questions.text: Proper nouns usually begins with _________
questions.explanation: Proper nouns always begin with capital letters.

questions.text: Common nouns are divided into _________
questions.explanation: Common nouns are classified as countable or uncountable.

questions.text: Examples of these can be regarded as proper nouns
questions.explanation: Mustapha, Yola, Monday, Christmas are specific names = proper nouns.

questions.text: Nouns can perform the following functions except
questions.explanation: Nouns do not function as complements of verbs (that's adjective/adverb role).

questions.text: The examples of verb in 'I am a king' and 'the water is cold' are
questions.explanation: 'am' and 'is' are linking verbs in these sentences.

questions.text: How many types of verbs do we have in English language?
questions.explanation: English verbs are classified into main (lexical) and auxiliary verbs.
options: Main or Lexical verbs and Auxiliary

questions.text: The main or lexical verbs are divided into smaller groups. They are:
questions.explanation: Main verbs分为 transitive, intransitive, and linking verbs.
options: Transitive verbs, action verbs, intransitive verbs, and linking verbs
Transitive verbs, intransitive verbs, and linking verbs
Transitive, intransitive linking and fragmented verbs

questions.text: Auxiliary verbs are subdivided into________ groups:
questions.explanation: Auxiliaries are divided into 2 groups: primary auxiliaries and modals.

questions.text: Primary auxiliary verbs and modal auxiliary verbs are examples of_______
questions.explanation: Both primary auxiliaries and modals are types of auxiliary verbs.

questions.text: English language has ________ parts of speech
questions.explanation: English has 8 parts of speech: noun, verb, adjective, adverb, preposition, conjunction, pronoun, interjection.

questions.text: Adjectives traditionally describe nouns or pronouns in sentences:
questions.explanation: Adjectives modify nouns and pronouns.

questions.text: The word 'Loudly' in the sentence 'He cried loudly' is an example of__________
questions.explanation: 'Loudly' modifies the verb 'cried', so it is an adverb.

questions.text: Adjectives have three degrees of comparison. They are:
questions.explanation: The three degrees are positive, comparative, and superlative.
options: The positive degree, the balance degree and the comparative degree
The positive degree, the comparative and the superlative degree

questions.text: The superlative degree of adjectives is used to express______
questions.explanation: Superlative is used when comparing three or more things.

questions.text: All of the following are examples of preposition except _____
questions.explanation: 'Neither, either, both, but' are conjunctions, not prepositions.

questions.text: Students are involved in two types of study. They are:
questions.explanation: Students engage in private (solo) and group study.

questions.text: For the student-learner to effectively organize his study time, certain points are considered:
questions.explanation: Effective study organization considers the learner, materials, and resources.
options: the individual as a learner only
materials for study and purpose of study
the individual as a learner, material for study, and materials available to student-learner

questions.text: The process of perceiving, recognizing, interpreting and understanding a sensible grammatical unity of a language is called____
questions.explanation: Reading involves perceiving, recognizing, interpreting and understanding text.

questions.text: Two main types of reading involve
questions.explanation: The two main types of reading are intensive and extensive.

questions.text: A language is generally a system of communication that is a_________ of symbols, a set of rules for making these symbols and a set of principles for interpretation of symbols
questions.explanation: A language is fundamentally a system of signs/symbols for communication.

questions.text: A language is a system of communication which consists of a set of symbols that is often referred to as a set of_________
questions.explanation: Language symbols are commonly referred to as signs.

questions.text: Human languages are also referred to as_________ languages
questions.explanation: Human languages are called natural languages.

questions.text: Sign languages are those used by _____________ individuals to communicate in the absence of the spoken language
questions.explanation: Sign languages are used by hearing-impaired individuals.

questions.text: Language is primarily a means of ___________
questions.explanation: The primary purpose of language is communication.

questions.text: Human language is generally described as an open system of communication. It is open in the sense that it is_________
questions.explanation: Language is open because it can generate infinite new expressions.

questions.text: Language is not just a means of communication but a complex system of human behavior. It is a social_________ which humans are born to acquire and use
questions.explanation: Language is part of human culture and social behavior.

questions.text: There are _________ basic properties of human language
questions.explanation: There are five basic properties of human language.

questions.text: The concept of ___________ implies the capacity of speakers to generate new utterances which have never been said before
questions.explanation: Productivity allows speakers to create unlimited new sentences.

questions.text: ______ is the arbitrary association of sounds and meanings in language
questions.explanation: Arbitrariness means there is no natural connection between sound and meaning.

questions.text: Arbitrariness is most easily seen in _________
questions.explanation: Arbitrariness is most evident in individual words.

questions.text: Language consists of two structural levels. This is called_________
questions.explanation: Duality means language operates at two structural levels (sounds and meaning).

questions.text: Language is described as _____________ because we can talk and write about events that are not present
questions.explanation: Displacement allows us to refer to things not in the immediate environment.

questions.text: The idea that _________ and meanings are both important to human communication is referred to as the double articulation of human language
questions.explanation: Double articulation involves both sounds (form) and meanings (content).

questions.text: Language is used to fulfill a lot of functions. Jakobson identifies __________ functions of language in human communication
questions.explanation: Jakobson identified 6 functions of language (referential, emotive, conative, phatic, metalingual, poetic).

questions.text: The function of language which is concerned with the social interaction of the speaker and the addressee is called the________ function
questions.explanation: The phatic function concerns social interaction and communication channels.

questions.text: The function of language which expresses the feeling or emotion of the speaker is called the__________ function
questions.explanation: The emotive function expresses the speaker's feelings and emotions.

questions.text: The ____________ function of language is also referred to as the context-based function of language
questions.explanation: The referential function relates to the context and information being conveyed.

questions.text: Which function of language is concerned with the message itself?
questions.explanation: The poetic function focuses on the message itself (aesthetic use of language).

questions.text: ________ function of language is used for the benefit of the addressee
questions.explanation: The conative function aims to influence or benefit the addressee.

questions.text: _________ function of language is concerned with the social interaction and relationship of the speaker and the addressee
questions.explanation: The phatic function maintains social bonds and relationships.

questions.text: Hymes' theory of communicative competence relates to the ability to use language for________
questions.explanation: Communicative competence encompasses all these aspects of language use.

questions.text: Hymes postulates the following points of view in his speech except
questions.explanation: Hymes focused on knowledge, language, and culture, not behaviour.

questions.text: Communication is the_____________ of meaning or information between a sender and a receiver
questions.explanation: Communication involves the exchange of meaning between sender and receiver.

questions.text: The following are principles of communication except
questions.explanation: Confusion is not a principle of communication; it is an obstacle.

questions.text: The following are principles of communication except one:
questions.explanation: Information is not a principle of communication - it is what is communicated.

questions.text: __________ is the process by which the source or sender of a message composes a message with the intention of passing it on
questions.explanation: Encoding is the process of composing/formulating a message for transmission.

questions.text: Language can be best understood if considered as a tool for_____________
questions.explanation: Language is best understood as a tool for communication.

questions.text: Communication is the _____________ of information between the sender and the receiver
questions.explanation: Communication is the exchange of information between sender and receiver.

questions.text: Communication involves_________ which are the sources, the sender, the message, the channel, the receiver and the target
questions.explanation: These are the key elements/components of the communication process.

questions.text: The language of the modern world is the _______________ of human culture
questions.explanation: Lingua Franca serves as the common language of human culture worldwide.

questions.text: Lingua Franca is the language of ____________
questions.explanation: Lingua Franca serves communication, culture, and the modern world.

questions.text: English is generally accepted as the __________ Lingua Franca of the world today
questions.explanation: English is the international Lingua Franca of the modern world.

questions.text: English has a very unique and interesting position in language ecology because it is native to very few countries
questions.explanation: English is native to several countries (UK, US, Australia, Canada, etc).

questions.text: Communication is based on feedback from the _______
questions.explanation: Communication relies on feedback from the audience/receiver.

questions.text: The ___________ of communication are the components that make up communication to occur and take effect
questions.explanation: Elements of communication are the components that enable communication.

questions.text: The _____________ is the initiator of communication
questions.explanation: The sender initiates the communication process.

questions.text: The _______________ is the information that is passed across in communication
questions.explanation: The message is the information being communicated.

questions.text: The ____________ is the channel through which the message is communicated to the receiver
questions.explanation: The channel is the medium through which the message travels.

questions.text: In communication, the ___________ is the person the message is intended for
questions.explanation: The receiver is the intended audience for the message.

questions.text: The ________________ is the response that the receiver gives after the message has been sent
questions.explanation: Feedback is the receiver's response to the message.

questions.text: Language is the __________ of the ____________ of human culture
questions.explanation: Language is the medium of the identity of human culture.

questions.text: Language can be described as a vehicle of cultural transmission
questions.explanation: Language serves as the vehicle for transmitting culture.

questions.text: The _________________ of culture is the process of transferring cultural values from one generation to the other
questions.explanation: Cultural transmission is the passing of cultural values across generations.

questions.text: Language is the primary vehicle for the ____________ of culture
questions.explanation: Language is the primary vehicle for cultural transmission.

questions.text: Language and culture are _____________
questions.explanation: Language and culture are deeply intertwined and inseparable.

questions.text: Language is _______________ for the existence of culture
questions.explanation: Language is essential for culture to exist and be transmitted.

questions.text: Language and culture have a ________________ relationship
questions.explanation: Language and culture have a mutual, reciprocal relationship.

questions.text: Language is the most _____________ form of culture
questions.explanation: Language is the most complex form of human culture.

questions.text: The ____________ of language is the most effective instrument for cultural transmission
questions.explanation: The spoken form of language is most effective for cultural transmission.

questions.text: Language is the ____________ of culture
questions.explanation: Language serves as the identity of culture.

questions.text: Culture is the _____________ of language
questions.explanation: Culture is the medium through which language operates.

questions.text: Language and culture are _____________ dependent on each other
questions.explanation: Language and culture are mutually dependent on each other.

questions.text: The relationship between language and culture is _____________
questions.explanation: The relationship between language and culture is complex and deep.

questions.text: Language is the _____________ that holds culture together
questions.explanation: Language is the glue that holds culture together.

questions.text: Culture is the _____________ that gives language meaning
questions.explanation: Culture provides the context that gives language its meaning.

questions.text: Language is the _____________ of human culture
questions.explanation: Language is the soul of human culture - its essential spirit.
